facts.text: Buffalo’s name comes from Buffalo Creek, a name that echoes the city’s early connection to the river and its surrounding wetlands.
Local inventor Joseph Dart and engineer Robert Dunbar built the first steam-powered grain elevator in 1843, revolutionizing bulk grain handling worldwide.
Buffalo was a key terminus of the Underground Railroad, where many free Black people crossed the Niagara River to Canada.
Lake‑effect snow turns Buffalo into a winter wonderland, with intense snowfall bands that depend on wind direction off Lake Erie.
The Freedom Wall, unveiled in 2017, honors civil‑rights activists and stands as a vibrant public art tribute in Buffalo.
